---
schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: 2ea3600c4f70504f07c28ebde6e47f08a41dbfc4
findings:
  - { id: judged-downgrade-cite-enforcement, kind: judged, text: "AC-5's 'every status downgrade carries a cite:' still bites only at PR time: TestGuideClaimsDowngrades_AgainstMergeBase diffs the working-tree manifest against docs/guide-claims.yaml at merge-base(HEAD, origin/main), so on main itself merge-base == HEAD and the manifest diffs against itself — a downgrade that reaches main un-caught is never retroactively flagged (disclosed at internal/specalign/guideclaims_test.go:1096-1107). The delete-and-recreate evasion has since been narrowed by findRenameDowngrades, but its 'cite documents the removal' test is substring containment of the removed row id inside any same-section cite (guideclaims_test.go:919), and a 'fresh cite' clearing an id-matched downgrade remains mere string inequality (findDowngradesWithoutFreshCite, guideclaims_test.go:848) — any shape-valid different string clears it with no check the citation is about the downgrade. On a branch where the manifest has no prior at the merge-base the gate is a loud skip, not a check. All residuals are disclosed in-repo and were adjudicated in the guide-claims-gate story's archived report; they persist at feature close. (confidence 0.50)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Reaffirmed at re-close: the wave narrowed the cluster (findRenameDowngrades landed, entry-anchored cites landed) and the remaining holes are disclosed in-repo; identity-stable row keys remain the deferred fix; standing archived ruling carried.", carried-from: 2ea3600c4f70504f07c28ebde6e47f08a41dbfc4 }
  - { id: judged-pass-coupling-gate-internal, kind: judged, text: "AC-5's third witness binding — 'PASS-coupled in make verify' — is proven by a fresh, gate-internal `go test -v -count=1 -run ^(names)$ ./...` transcript checked through scripts/require-pass.sh (runGuideClaimWitnessTranscript, internal/specalign/guideclaims_test.go:357), not by coupling to make verify's own gate invocations: the inner run omits -race (which make test uses) and the named witnesses execute more than once per verify. Coupling to verify holds only transitively (the gate runs under spec-align, a verify prerequisite). The intent — a skipped, build-tag-excluded, or never-invoked witness reds (vacuous `go test -run` exit 0 is exactly what require-pass.sh catches) — is achieved and tested, so this is a letter-level divergence, disclosed in the package doc (guideclaims_test.go:59-78) and adjudicated in the guide-claims-gate story; it persists at feature close. (confidence 0.35)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Reaffirmed: standing disclosed letter-level divergence, intent achieved and pinned; stable across six reports.", carried-from: 2ea3600c4f70504f07c28ebde6e47f08a41dbfc4 }
  - { id: judged-design-mode-ac1-gap, kind: judged, text: "AC-1's letter ('judge-backed verbs ... print their report path as the first line of stdout, write the report atomically, and support a bounded --wait') is scoped by adjudication to align's build mode plus close's freeze-align, but `verdi align` on a design/ branch runs the same long judge exchange (align.GenerateDecisionConflict) while writing decision-conflict-report.md through raw os.WriteFile (cmd/verdi/align_design.go:128), printing the path only in the post-write summary, and --wait is refused for this mode. A reader polling the path mid-run can observe a partial write. The gap is disclosed at the write site (the DISCLOSURE block immediately above it, align_design.go:113-126) and was adjudicated in the judge-ergonomics story's archived report; AC-1's parenthetical does not qualify by mode, so the letter-level deviation persists at feature close pending the accepted ratification amendment. (confidence 0.35)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Reaffirmed: disclosed at site; ac-1's mode qualification is in the corrected ratification record (08 §2, feature ac-1) awaiting the amendment commit.", carried-from: 2ea3600c4f70504f07c28ebde6e47f08a41dbfc4 }
  - { id: judged-diagram-sweep-ac1-gap, kind: judged, text: "align --diagram-sweep is also judge-backed and shows the same AC-1 gap shape as design mode with weaker disclosure: raw os.WriteFile for sweep-report.md (cmd/verdi/aligndiagramsweep.go:108), report path printed only in the completion summary, and --wait rejected for the mode. The --wait scoping-out is disclosed in align.go's package comment, but the per-site AC-1 disclosure comment scheduled for the endgame wave has still not landed — grep of aligndiagramsweep.go finds no disclosure covering the non-atomic write or the missing first-line path, and no story-level finding adjudicated this mode by name. Mitigating: the sweep report is a disposable advisory artifact never read by gate, lint, or CI (stated in the verb's own output), outside X-8's closure-blocking problem statement. (confidence 0.35)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Reaffirmed WITH A RECORDED MISS: the per-site disclosure comment was adjudicated into the endgame wave but dropped from every fix-agent dispatch — the judge caught the dropped commitment. Re-recorded as an explicit named residual (2-line comment, lands in the next commit window: the ratification commits or feature 2's first PR); mitigation stands (advisory artifact, no gate consumer).", carried-from: 2ea3600c4f70504f07c28ebde6e47f08a41dbfc4 }
  - { id: judged-capability-row-atomicity, kind: judged, text: "AC-5's atomic-capability-row guarantee is structural only: DecodeGuideClaims rejects a list-valued capability and sub_claims at decode, but a single scalar capability: string can and does describe several capabilities under one status — docs/guide-claims.yaml row 2.1-local-first-serve-and-dex reads 'Local-first serve, writer lock, sharing-is-committing, dex on Pages' under one EXISTS, and sibling rows (2.2's 'Design/build/closure MRs; CODEOWNERS as countersign; merge-is-closure' among others) share the shape. Outside the sections pinned by the adjudicated sub-row tables (findAdjudicatedSubRowMismatches, internal/specalign/guideclaims_transcription_test.go), nothing machine-checks that a row is semantically one capability, so 'rejected at decode' is true only of the row shape. Disclosed in the manifest header as the L-N4 display-grouping adjudication and surfaced in the story's archived report; persists at feature close. (confidence 0.30)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Reaffirmed: L-N4 display-grouping adjudication; semantic atomicity pinned for adjudicated sections; full pairing awaits Task 18.", carried-from: 2ea3600c4f70504f07c28ebde6e47f08a41dbfc4 }
digest: sha256:5adbc73576c787197e23506d5e21c79ef477c046726e7856c072cfd7b240cb62
integrity: sha256:f7d146bd8ddc9ce9d7599c059c18af79307179951e14c5862b451dae2a23b79f
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"downgrade-cite-enforcement\",\"text\":\"AC-5's 'every status downgrade carries a cite:' still bites only at PR time: TestGuideClaimsDowngrades_AgainstMergeBase diffs the working-tree manifest against docs/guide-claims.yaml at merge-base(HEAD, origin/main), so on main itself merge-base == HEAD and the manifest diffs against itself — a downgrade that reaches main un-caught is never retroactively flagged (disclosed at internal/specalign/guideclaims_test.go:1096-1107). The delete-and-recreate evasion has since been narrowed by findRenameDowngrades, but its 'cite documents the removal' test is substring containment of the removed row id inside any same-section cite (guideclaims_test.go:919), and a 'fresh cite' clearing an id-matched downgrade remains mere string inequality (findDowngradesWithoutFreshCite, guideclaims_test.go:848) — any shape-valid different string clears it with no check the citation is about the downgrade. On a branch where the manifest has no prior at the merge-base the gate is a loud skip, not a check. All residuals are disclosed in-repo and were adjudicated in the guide-claims-gate story's archived report; they persist at feature close.\",\"confidence\":0.5},{\"id\":\"pass-coupling-gate-internal\",\"text\":\"AC-5's third witness binding — 'PASS-coupled in make verify' — is proven by a fresh, gate-internal `go test -v -count=1 -run ^(names)$ ./...` transcript checked through scripts/require-pass.sh (runGuideClaimWitnessTranscript, internal/specalign/guideclaims_test.go:357), not by coupling to make verify's own gate invocations: the inner run omits -race (which make test uses) and the named witnesses execute more than once per verify. Coupling to verify holds only transitively (the gate runs under spec-align, a verify prerequisite). The intent — a skipped, build-tag-excluded, or never-invoked witness reds (vacuous `go test -run` exit 0 is exactly what require-pass.sh catches) — is achieved and tested, so this is a letter-level divergence, disclosed in the package doc (guideclaims_test.go:59-78) and adjudicated in the guide-claims-gate story; it persists at feature close.\",\"confidence\":0.35},{\"id\":\"design-mode-ac1-gap\",\"text\":\"AC-1's letter ('judge-backed verbs ... print their report path as the first line of stdout, write the report atomically, and support a bounded --wait') is scoped by adjudication to align's build mode plus close's freeze-align, but `verdi align` on a design/ branch runs the same long judge exchange (align.GenerateDecisionConflict) while writing decision-conflict-report.md through raw os.WriteFile (cmd/verdi/align_design.go:128), printing the path only in the post-write summary, and --wait is refused for this mode. A reader polling the path mid-run can observe a partial write. The gap is disclosed at the write site (the DISCLOSURE block immediately above it, align_design.go:113-126) and was adjudicated in the judge-ergonomics story's archived report; AC-1's parenthetical does not qualify by mode, so the letter-level deviation persists at feature close pending the accepted ratification amendment.\",\"confidence\":0.35},{\"id\":\"diagram-sweep-ac1-gap\",\"text\":\"align --diagram-sweep is also judge-backed and shows the same AC-1 gap shape as design mode with weaker disclosure: raw os.WriteFile for sweep-report.md (cmd/verdi/aligndiagramsweep.go:108), report path printed only in the completion summary, and --wait rejected for the mode. The --wait scoping-out is disclosed in align.go's package comment, but the per-site AC-1 disclosure comment scheduled for the endgame wave has still not landed — grep of aligndiagramsweep.go finds no disclosure covering the non-atomic write or the missing first-line path, and no story-level finding adjudicated this mode by name. Mitigating: the sweep report is a disposable advisory artifact never read by gate, lint, or CI (stated in the verb's own output), outside X-8's closure-blocking problem statement.\",\"confidence\":0.35},{\"id\":\"capability-row-atomicity\",\"text\":\"AC-5's atomic-capability-row guarantee is structural only: DecodeGuideClaims rejects a list-valued capability and sub_claims at decode, but a single scalar capability: string can and does describe several capabilities under one status — docs/guide-claims.yaml row 2.1-local-first-serve-and-dex reads 'Local-first serve, writer lock, sharing-is-committing, dex on Pages' under one EXISTS, and sibling rows (2.2's 'Design/build/closure MRs; CODEOWNERS as countersign; merge-is-closure' among others) share the shape. Outside the sections pinned by the adjudicated sub-row tables (findAdjudicatedSubRowMismatches, internal/specalign/guideclaims_transcription_test.go), nothing machine-checks that a row is semantically one capability, so 'rejected at decode' is true only of the row shape. Disclosed in the manifest header as the L-N4 display-grouping adjudication and surfaced in the story's archived report; persists at feature close.\",\"confidence\":0.3}]}" }
frozen: { at: 2026-07-21, commit: 2ea3600c4f70504f07c28ebde6e47f08a41dbfc4 }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v0, inputs: [spec/ritual-integrity@2ea3600c4f70504f07c28ebde6e47f08a41dbfc4, spec/ritual-integrity@59cade5f8f69f7c52543ee277a2205e778caa6bb], digest: sha256:5adbc73576c787197e23506d5e21c79ef477c046726e7856c072cfd7b240cb62, integrity: sha256:f7d146bd8ddc9ce9d7599c059c18af79307179951e14c5862b451dae2a23b79f, model: sha256:a8b2e3c1eb4defba461355077a2b82ae13db212beaad6e78eab05db51e5b6721 }
---
# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-downgrade-cite-enforcement** [accepted-deviation]: AC-5's 'every status downgrade carries a cite:' still bites only at PR time: TestGuideClaimsDowngrades_AgainstMergeBase diffs the working-tree manifest against docs/guide-claims.yaml at merge-base(HEAD, origin/main), so on main itself merge-base == HEAD and the manifest diffs against itself — a downgrade that reaches main un-caught is never retroactively flagged (disclosed at internal/specalign/guideclaims_test.go:1096-1107). The delete-and-recreate evasion has since been narrowed by findRenameDowngrades, but its 'cite documents the removal' test is substring containment of the removed row id inside any same-section cite (guideclaims_test.go:919), and a 'fresh cite' clearing an id-matched downgrade remains mere string inequality (findDowngradesWithoutFreshCite, guideclaims_test.go:848) — any shape-valid different string clears it with no check the citation is about the downgrade. On a branch where the manifest has no prior at the merge-base the gate is a loud skip, not a check. All residuals are disclosed in-repo and were adjudicated in the guide-claims-gate story's archived report; they persist at feature close. (confidence 0.50) — Reaffirmed at re-close: the wave narrowed the cluster (findRenameDowngrades landed, entry-anchored cites landed) and the remaining holes are disclosed in-repo; identity-stable row keys remain the deferred fix; standing archived ruling carried.
- **judged-pass-coupling-gate-internal** [accepted-deviation]: AC-5's third witness binding — 'PASS-coupled in make verify' — is proven by a fresh, gate-internal `go test -v -count=1 -run ^(names)$ ./...` transcript checked through scripts/require-pass.sh (runGuideClaimWitnessTranscript, internal/specalign/guideclaims_test.go:357), not by coupling to make verify's own gate invocations: the inner run omits -race (which make test uses) and the named witnesses execute more than once per verify. Coupling to verify holds only transitively (the gate runs under spec-align, a verify prerequisite). The intent — a skipped, build-tag-excluded, or never-invoked witness reds (vacuous `go test -run` exit 0 is exactly what require-pass.sh catches) — is achieved and tested, so this is a letter-level divergence, disclosed in the package doc (guideclaims_test.go:59-78) and adjudicated in the guide-claims-gate story; it persists at feature close. (confidence 0.35) — Reaffirmed: standing disclosed letter-level divergence, intent achieved and pinned; stable across six reports.
- **judged-design-mode-ac1-gap** [accepted-deviation]: AC-1's letter ('judge-backed verbs ... print their report path as the first line of stdout, write the report atomically, and support a bounded --wait') is scoped by adjudication to align's build mode plus close's freeze-align, but `verdi align` on a design/ branch runs the same long judge exchange (align.GenerateDecisionConflict) while writing decision-conflict-report.md through raw os.WriteFile (cmd/verdi/align_design.go:128), printing the path only in the post-write summary, and --wait is refused for this mode. A reader polling the path mid-run can observe a partial write. The gap is disclosed at the write site (the DISCLOSURE block immediately above it, align_design.go:113-126) and was adjudicated in the judge-ergonomics story's archived report; AC-1's parenthetical does not qualify by mode, so the letter-level deviation persists at feature close pending the accepted ratification amendment. (confidence 0.35) — Reaffirmed: disclosed at site; ac-1's mode qualification is in the corrected ratification record (08 §2, feature ac-1) awaiting the amendment commit.
- **judged-diagram-sweep-ac1-gap** [accepted-deviation]: align --diagram-sweep is also judge-backed and shows the same AC-1 gap shape as design mode with weaker disclosure: raw os.WriteFile for sweep-report.md (cmd/verdi/aligndiagramsweep.go:108), report path printed only in the completion summary, and --wait rejected for the mode. The --wait scoping-out is disclosed in align.go's package comment, but the per-site AC-1 disclosure comment scheduled for the endgame wave has still not landed — grep of aligndiagramsweep.go finds no disclosure covering the non-atomic write or the missing first-line path, and no story-level finding adjudicated this mode by name. Mitigating: the sweep report is a disposable advisory artifact never read by gate, lint, or CI (stated in the verb's own output), outside X-8's closure-blocking problem statement. (confidence 0.35) — Reaffirmed WITH A RECORDED MISS: the per-site disclosure comment was adjudicated into the endgame wave but dropped from every fix-agent dispatch — the judge caught the dropped commitment. Re-recorded as an explicit named residual (2-line comment, lands in the next commit window: the ratification commits or feature 2's first PR); mitigation stands (advisory artifact, no gate consumer).
- **judged-capability-row-atomicity** [accepted-deviation]: AC-5's atomic-capability-row guarantee is structural only: DecodeGuideClaims rejects a list-valued capability and sub_claims at decode, but a single scalar capability: string can and does describe several capabilities under one status — docs/guide-claims.yaml row 2.1-local-first-serve-and-dex reads 'Local-first serve, writer lock, sharing-is-committing, dex on Pages' under one EXISTS, and sibling rows (2.2's 'Design/build/closure MRs; CODEOWNERS as countersign; merge-is-closure' among others) share the shape. Outside the sections pinned by the adjudicated sub-row tables (findAdjudicatedSubRowMismatches, internal/specalign/guideclaims_transcription_test.go), nothing machine-checks that a row is semantically one capability, so 'rejected at decode' is true only of the row shape. Disclosed in the manifest header as the L-N4 display-grouping adjudication and surfaced in the story's archived report; persists at feature close. (confidence 0.30) — Reaffirmed: L-N4 display-grouping adjudication; semantic atomicity pinned for adjudicated sections; full pairing awaits Task 18.

### Candidates awaiting reaffirmation

(none)

## Not resurfaced

(none)
